successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY), LeaveRequestStatus (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED), and LeaveRequest with fields id, employeeId, leaveType, and status.

src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository with methods create(request: LeaveRequest): Promise<void>, findById(id: string): Promise<LeaveRequest | null>, and updateStatus(id: string, status: LeaveRequestStatus): Promise<void>.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exports PgLeaveRequestRepository implementing the repository contract using LeaveRequest model types.
A Vitest unit test exists under tests/unit/modules/leave/ and validates LeaveRequest type usage and repository contract compatibility.
All imports between the leave model, repository contract, and tests resolve correctly and compile under TypeScript using only artifacts introduced in this phase.
Project architecture documentation is updated to record LeaveType values (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY) and LeaveRequestStatus values (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED).

constraints: Limit implementation to type definitions, DTO/types, repository interfaces, repository contracts, tests, and documentation updates described in this phase.
Repository pattern: all persistence access must be expressed through repository interfaces; no direct database access implementation beyond contract definitions.
Define PostgreSQL-facing repository contracts only; do not implement persistence logic.
Use canonical LeaveRequest attributes: id, employeeId, leaveType, status.
Repository contracts must consume and return types defined in src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts.

Input validation, RBAC enforcement, audit-record writing, and async error-handling concerns are not implemented in this phase because no API or service behavior is introduced, but future architecture must remain compatible with these platform principles.
No sensitive data should be logged if any test or placeholder code is added.
outOfScope: AuditRecord domain model and repository contracts.
API routes, controllers, RBAC enforcement, or request validation logic.
Employee domain model and repository contracts.
Leave balance and leave policy models or repositories.
Notification domain model, repositories, or notification services.
Database schema migrations or actual PostgreSQL persistence implementation.
Any functionality beyond LeaveRequest model types, repository contracts, tests, and related documentation updates.

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions defining submission DTO/types, but the detailed architecture only specifies LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest.
options: Create only the explicitly defined types in the architecture specification.
Add a separate submission DTO type in leave.model.ts in addition to the specified LeaveRequest types.

ambiguities.description: The location and format of the architecture documentation update are not specified.
options: Update an existing architecture.md or equivalent project architecture document.
Create or update a module-level documentation artifact describing LeaveType and LeaveRequestStatus values.
